perturbation.original_text: This Strategic Alliance Agreement ("Agreement") is executed on this 11th day of December, 2015 ("Execution Date") by and between ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES INC., a company incorporated under the laws of Taiwan ("ChipMOS"), and Tsinghua Unigroup Ltd. ("Tsinghua Unigroup"), a company incorporated under the laws of the People's Republic of China ("PRC"). ChipMOS and Tsinghua Unigroup shall collectively be referred to as the "Parties."
perturbation.changed_text: This Business Partnership Agreement ("Agreement") is executed on this 11th day of December, 2015 ("Execution Date") by and between ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES INC., a company incorporated under the laws of Taiwan ("ChipMOS"), and Tsinghua Unigroup Ltd. ("Tsinghua Unigroup"), a company incorporated under the laws of the People's Republic of China ("PRC"). ChipMOS and Tsinghua Unigroup shall collectively be referred to as the "Parties."
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Strategic Alliance Agreement' to 'Business Partnership Agreement' creates confusion. Throughout the document, the agreement is consistently referred to as a 'Strategic Alliance Agreement.' This perturbation introduces an inconsistency in the fundamental nature of the agreement, contradicting all subsequent references to it as a 'Strategic Alliance Agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 1 Strategic Alliance 1.1 Content of Strategic Alliance and Expected Benefits After the Closing Date (as defined in the Share Subscription Agreement), Tsinghua Unigroup and ChipMOS shall cooperate, expand, strengthen and stabilize the relationship with the related upstream, midstream, and downstream industries engaged in the assembly and testing services of LCD drivers, microelectromechanical systems (MEMS), the Internet of Things (IoT) and Radio Frequency Integrated Circuits (RFIC) and/or wafer bumping services in Mainland China. Tsinghua Unigroup shall also introduce other potential suppliers, customers and business partners in Mainland China to ChipMOS. - 1 - Confidential (Translation, for reference only) 1.2 Covenants of Parties (1) Tsinghua Unigroup covenants to follow the Share Subscription Agreement to subscribe for, via the Subscriber, in compliance with the requirements of Taiwan's laws and regulations relating to securities transactions and PRC investment in Taiwan, at the Subscription Price per Share (as defined in the Share Subscription Agreement), 299,252,000 common shares through private placement from ChipMOS, and Tsinghua Unigroup shall comply with, and shall cause the Subscriber to comply with the content of the Share Subscription Agreement, Taiwan's laws and regulations concerning securities transactions and PRC investments in Taiwan so that ChipMOS may make use of the Total Subscription Price (as defined in the Share Subscription Agreement) to replenish operating capital, recruit talents, and upgrade its technologies related to the semiconductor assembly and testing services, to create profits for each of the Parties and its shareholders. (2) ChipMOS covenants that part or all of the Total Subscription Price shall be used: (a) To strengthen research and development, and technologies, and expand production capacity in Taiwan in order to strengthen itsroots in Taiwan, and increase job opportunities. (b) To increase the capital of ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES (Shanghai) LTD. ("ChipMOS Shanghai"), and replenish the operating capital of ChipMOS Shanghai, in order to expand ChipMOS and its affiliates' business scale in LCD driver and Specialty Memory IC assembly and testing services and/or wafer bumping services markets, and thus increase ChipMOS' global market share. (c) As funds for the merger with ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES (Bermuda) LTD. (d) As funds for the merger and acquisition by ChipMOS of other appropriate targets in the semiconductor industry in Taiwanwhich have similar ideals, share a common goal, and are industrially complimentary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text renames the 'Strategic Alliance Agreement' to 'Business Partnership Agreement'. The contradicted text explicitly references 'Strategic Alliance' multiple times, including 'Article 1 Strategic Alliance'. This direct name change creates a contradiction because the contradicted text now refers to an agreement that, according to the changed text, no longer exists by that name. This falls under Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, where using different terms for the same thing leads to conflicting obligations and confusion.

perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Implementation of Strategic Alliance Each of the Parties covenants to, after the Closing Date, designate related staff to hold regular meetings to propose a specific plan and schedule in connection with Sections 1.1 and 1.2 herein, perform the specific plan together and review the implementation status. Each Party shall use its reasonable best efforts to provide immediate assistance to, and actively cooperate with, the other Party, to implement this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Implementation of Strategic Endeavor Each of the Parties covenants to, after the Closing Date, designate related staff to hold regular meetings to propose a specific plan and schedule in connection with Sections 1.1 and 1.2 herein, perform the specific plan together and review the implementation status. Each Party shall use its reasonable best efforts to provide immediate assistance to, and actively cooperate with, the other Party, to implement this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Strategic Alliance' to 'Strategic Endeavor' within Section 1.3 introduces inconsistency. While the subsequent sentence refers to implementing 'this Agreement' (which is still defined at the beginning), the change in terminology creates ambiguity about whether Section 1.3 is still directly tied to the main 'Strategic Alliance Agreement' as defined initially. This is going to create an In-Text Contradiction within the same paragraph
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Implementation of Strategic Endeavor Each of the Parties covenants to, after the Closing Date, designate related staff to hold regular meetings to propose a specific plan and schedule in connection with Sections 1.1 and 1.2 herein, perform the specific plan together and review the implementation status. Each Party shall use its reasonable best efforts to provide immediate assistance to, and actively cooperate with, the other Party, to implement this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Strategic Alliance' to 'Strategic Endeavor' in Section 1.3, while seemingly minor, introduces ambiguity regarding the specific agreement being implemented. The contradicted text (which is the changed text itself) now refers to 'Strategic Endeavor,' but the rest of the agreement, including references in sections 1.1 and 1.2, may still be tied to the defined 'Strategic Alliance Agreement.' This misaligned terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, creates a contradiction because it is now unclear whether Section 1.3 pertains to the original 'Strategic Alliance' or to a separate 'Strategic Endeavor.' The fact that the paragraph still refers to implementing 'this Agreement' does not override the created ambiguity regarding the relationship to the Strategic Alliance Agreement as initially defined, therefore directly contradicting the intent of the original text and creating ambiguity where there was certainty. This aligns with 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.9 Confidentiality The Parties agree that the Parties will not disclose information in connection with the execution, existence, content, and performance of this Agreement to any third party before the Parties have made an announcement to the public pursuant to Section 3.11 of this Agreement. However, the foregoing restriction shall not apply to disclosure made to the board of the directors, management team, and relevant employees who need to know such information, attorneys, accountants, financial counsel, and competent authorities for the purposes of performing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.9 Secrecy The Parties agree that the Parties will not disclose information in connection with the execution, existence, content, and performance of this strategic partnership to any third party before the Parties have made an announcement to the public pursuant to Section 3.11 of this Agreement. However, the foregoing restriction shall not apply to disclosure made to the board of the directors, management team, and relevant employees who need to know such information, attorneys, accountants, financial counsel, and competent authorities for the purposes of performing this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidentiality' with 'Secrecy' and changing 'this Agreement' to 'this strategic partnership' in Section 3.9 creates a contradiction. While the paragraph discusses disclosure restrictions related to what is established in the initial agreement. This leads to uncertainty, which has an in-text contradiction in the same paragraph since the 'strategic partnership' term has never been mentioned before, there is no definition for it
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.9 Secrecy The Parties agree that the Parties will not disclose information in connection with the execution, existence, content, and performance of this strategic partnership to any third party before the Parties have made an announcement to the public pursuant to Section 3.11 of this Agreement. However, the foregoing restriction shall not apply to disclosure made to the board of the directors, management team, and relevant employees who need to know such information, attorneys, accountants, financial counsel, and competent authorities for the purposes of performing this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the term 'strategic partnership' without defining it, while the original text referred to 'this Agreement.' This creates a contradiction because the contradicted section, which is also the changed text, now refers to a term that has no prior definition or context within the agreement itself. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as it introduces a new term without defining it and uses it interchangeably, which can lead to conflicting obligations.
